headline: Deputy ministers attend only 34% of parliamentary question sessions
context: An analysis of Hansard records showed deputy ministers attended only 34% of oral question time sessions assigned to their portfolios in 2025.

cards.big: Deputy ministers showed up to only 34% of their question time sessions
cards.sub: Parliament's accountability mechanism undermined by simple absenteeism

cards.big: Standing Order 23 requires ministers to answer but has no attendance enforcement
cards.sub: Written replies are substituted without consequence when ministers skip sessions

cards.big: Each deputy minister costs RM540,000 annually in salary and allowances
cards.sub: Taxpayers pay for parliamentary accountability that is not being delivered

cards.big: Backbenchers' attendance averaged 61% — nearly double deputy ministers' rate
cards.sub: Those being held accountable show up less than those doing the holding

cards.big: A minister who won't face questions isn't governing — they're avoiding governance
Should ministerial pay be docked for unexcused absence from parliamentary sessions?
